name: Spc Brooklyn
description_short: SPC Brooklyn Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit credit union established in 1968, serving over 300 members in Brooklyn with savings accounts, personal loans, and auto loans.
description_long:
SPC Brooklyn Federal Credit Union was established in 1968 and is headquartered in Brooklyn, New York. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a not-for-profit financial institution focused on serving its member community. The institution has maintained a single branch location at 29 Claver Place in Brooklyn and stewards over $380,600 in assets.

The credit union offers a range of personal banking services including savings accounts, share draft accounts, money market accounts, share certificates (CDs), personal loans, and auto loans. Members have access to online banking and mobile app platforms for account management. The institution provides financial calculators and educational resources to help members make informed financial decisions. As a not-for-profit entity, SPC Brooklyn positions itself as offering competitive interest rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks.

SPC Brooklyn distinguishes itself through its specialization in serving members of religious groups, aligning financial services with spiritual values and community needs. This membership eligibility focus creates a tight-knit community of over 300 loyal members. The credit union emphasizes personalized service through its community-oriented approach and commitment to member education through tools like financial calculators and regular financial tips.

However, potential members should be aware of significant operational concerns. Recent reviews report that the physical Brooklyn, NY location may not be operational, with complaints that "there is no SPC Federal credit Union Brooklyn, NY location" and that the phone number (718) 857-5543 is non-working. The credit union currently shows 0 ATMs and 0 dedicated employees as of 2026, raising questions about actual service capacity. These factors suggest operational challenges that warrant verification before attempting to join or conduct business with the institution. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Not-for-profit structure offers competitive interest rates and lower fees than for-profit banks
Established institution with 56+ years of history since 1968
Membership eligibility focused on religious groups creates a values-aligned community
Online banking and mobile app access available for members
Offers multiple account types including savings, money market, and CDs
Personal loans and auto loans available for qualifying members
Financial calculators and educational resources provided to members
cons: Recent member reviews report the Brooklyn location is non-operational and phone number is non-working
Only 1 branch with 0 ATMs, severely limiting accessibility and service options
Very small asset base ($380,600) and limited membership (300+ members) may restrict service capacity
Membership restricted to religious group members, limiting eligibility for general population
No documented employees as of 2026, raising serious questions about operational viability
best_for: Members of eligible religious groups seeking community-focused banking aligned with spiritual values
Long-time members already established with the credit union seeking to maintain accounts
Individuals interested in credit union membership with specialized community focus
diagnosis: SPC Brooklyn FCU may be best suited for existing or prospective members of eligible religious communities, but serious operational concerns require verification before joining. Multiple recent reports indicate the main Brooklyn location may not be functional and contact information appears non-working, suggesting the institution may not be actively accepting new members or providing reliable service.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
